on either side of the per-rank slice, so loading any LoRA adapter that
targets qk

Problem
run_files in python/sglang/test/ci/ci_utils.py decides whether a
test file passed by reading the wrapping python script's
process.returncode. Files using
if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__])

discard pytest's exit code, so the script returns 0 even when assertions
fail. The

Fix --enable-nsa-prefill-context-parallel crash on dsv4-rebase.
dsv4-rebase introduced _ATTN_CP process group via attn_cp_size, but CP operations still used get_attention_tp_* (pointing to _ATTN_TP which shrinks to size 1 when CP is active). This caused shape mismatches in metadata, compressor allga

sglang.author: sihan-zzz

Motivation
--disable-radix-cache does two things at once:

swaps the cache class from RadixCache/SWARadixCache to ChunkCache/SWAChunkCache, and
as a side effect, removes any cross-request prefix reuse (because ChunkCache's match_prefix always returns empty).

For some use cases we want only (2), not

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
LoRARef.lora_id is generated with uuid4() (see lora_registry.py). When --lora-paths is parsed in ServerArgs.check_lora_server_args(), every node in a multi-node launch parses CLI args independently, so each rank mints a different lora_id for the same (lora_name, lora_path) pair.
The maste

The underlying error is in general, codegen does not work for unqualified names - this applies to cases like device as well.  In part

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Rocm]Aiter MoE re-uses existing tensor addresses after weight update.
vllm.author: yuankaichen-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The AMD aiter FusedMoE requires model weights be shuffled before use. When using this feature in RL scenario, however, the MoE weights will be re-shuffled time and again whenever a weight update is carried out.
The issue is that the weight shuffle should NOT change the tensor addresses -- otherwise

vllm.title: Fix some legacy checkpoints with deprecated `rope_type` values
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Make it clearer what patch_rope_parameters_dict (now called patch_legacy_rope_type) does.
Previously it was unclear that it was not a suitable substitute for config.standardize_rope_params() and config.validate_rope(). This was identified in #41599 where an iteration of the PR disabled standardisati

vllm.title: [CPU][RISC-V] Auto-bind OMP threads and harden nobind path
vllm.author: lyd1992
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Three related fixes for OMPProcessManager that unblock CPU worker init on RISC-V:


Auto-bind on RISC-V — extend the ARM branch in _parse_omp_threads_bind_env() and reserve_cpu_num to also cover CpuArchEnum.RISCV. RISC-V has no SMT, so the ARM logic ("use all logical CPUs") applies verbatim.

vllm.title: [MM][Gemma4] Respect max_soft_tokens in encoder budget
vllm.author: lesj0610
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Gemma 4 supports explicit numeric max_soft_tokens (70, 140, 280, 560, 1120) through mm_processor_kwargs. Runtime processing already respects this, but get_mm_max_tokens_per_item() always returns 280 regardless of config.
For smaller values like 70, this only wastes memory. But for 560 or 112

This fixes a forced-tool-choice crash when reasoning extraction returns content=None.
Some reasoning parsers, including the glm45 / DeepSeekV3ReasoningWithThinkingParser path, may consume the full model output and return (reasoning, None). The forced tool-choice branches in both chat-complet

vllm.title: [KV Offload] Return None from lookup() for in-flight blocks
vllm.author: ronensc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
CPUOffloadingManager.lookup() was returning False for both absent blocks and in-flight blocks (write started but not yet complete).
The base class defines None to mean "retry later" which is the right semantic for in-flight.
This makes the scheduler defer instead of treating an in-flight blo

vllm.author: lucianommartins
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) speculative decoding support for Gemma 4 assistant models. This enables draft-based speculative decoding for all Gemma 4 model variants (E2B, E4B, 26B-A4B, 31B) using their corresponding assistant checkpoints.
The Gemma 4 assistant is a lightweight decoder wi

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix spawn_new_process_for_each_test silently swallowing test failures
vllm.author: dzhengAP
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
spawn_new_process_for_each_test was broken — it always passed regardless
of what the test function did, causing silent test coverage failures.
The previous implementation ran python -m <module_name> in the child
process, which re-executed the module's __main__ block instead of
actually calli

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Account for truncate_prompt_tokens when computing max_tokens
vllm.author: viktorpusTT
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
When a request sets truncate_prompt_tokens, the prompt is truncated before inference but get_max_tokens() was receiving the original (untruncated) prompt length. This caused the output token budget to be underestimated by (original_len - truncated_len) tokens. Fix: compute input_length as mi

The aiter kernel now has support for q_scale

Purpose
External memset is introduced in #41444 but only when need_cooperative is true. need_cooperative only trigger when max_seq_len is greater than a certain threshold. And this means that the trigger will never fire at cuda graph capture time.
This PR remove need_cooperative statement so that th

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Remove `TORCH_NCCL_BLOCKING_WAIT=1` After Bugfix In ROCm 7.2
vllm.author: micah-wil
vllm.bodyExcerpt: As of ROCm 7.2, the TORCH_NCCL_BLOCKING_WAIT=1 workaround is no longer needed for distributed test groups on ROCm. The fix here: ROCm/rocm-systems#2177 was merged to address the issue tracked here ROCm/hip#3876. Here is a CI build with the affected TGs which show them passing without TORCH_NCCL_BLOC

vllm.title: [Docs] add cache directory security guidance
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Document that vLLM cache directories are assumed private and trusted,
and that providing untrusted access could result in crashes or arbitrary
code execution. List the relevant environment variables that control
cache locations.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com
